name: apply-modifiers
description: Modifier keywords that can follow a 'zone face apply' condition value to vary it over space, time, FISH callbacks, or convergence response.

modifiers.purpose: Apply a spatial gradient to scalar values.
notes: Always specify an origin when the default origin would make the gradient ambiguous.

modifiers.purpose: Multiply the base value by a FISH function return value each step.
notes: The FISH function should return a scalar or vector compatible with the applied condition.

modifiers.purpose: Evaluate a FISH multiplier separately for each face or gridpoint affected by the condition.
notes: For face-based conditions, the function can use the zone pointer and side number.

modifiers.purpose: Control the applied magnitude based on mechanical convergence ratio.
notes: Use ramp to increase load gradually; use reduce to remove a condition gradually.

modifiers.purpose: Help local-axis boundary logic respect specified symmetry planes.
notes: Most useful for velocity, acceleration, and reaction conditions based on local axes.

modifiers.purpose: Vary a condition through a table-controlled multiplier.
notes: Useful for staged loading, histories, hydrographs, and dynamic input records.
